task_completion.description: Open the 'Fly Brain' sample in Fiji
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji application window is open displaying an image titled 'flybrain' or 'Fly Brain', typically showing a multi-channel (red/green) confocal stack.

task_completion.description: Create and save a Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'fly_brain_MIP.tif' is visible in the ~/ImageJ_Data/results/ directory (via file manager or terminal), or a Fiji save dialog is shown saving this exact filename. A window titled 'MAX_flybrain' may also be visible.

task_completion.description: Create and save an Average Intensity Projection (AVG)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'fly_brain_AVG.tif' is visible in the ~/ImageJ_Data/results/ directory, or a Fiji save dialog is shown saving this exact filename. A window titled 'AVG_flybrain' may also be visible.

task_completion.description: Perform an Orthogonal Reslice to create an XZ view and save it
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'fly_brain_reslice_XZ.tif' is visible in the ~/ImageJ_Data/results/ directory. Evidence of the reslice operation (e.g., a window titled 'Reslice of flybrain' showing a top-down/XZ cross-section) should be present.

task_completion.description: Create a Montage showing a subset of slices and save it
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'fly_brain_montage.tif' is visible in the ~/ImageJ_Data/results/ directory. A Fiji window showing a grid of brain slices (Montage) should be visible during the process.

task_completion.description: Measure the mean intensity across the z-depth and save as CSV
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'fly_brain_z_profile.csv' is visible in the ~/ImageJ_Data/results/ directory. A Fiji 'Results' table or 'Plot' window showing intensity values across ~57 slices should be visible during the process.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Fiji GUI to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Fiji application interface, menus (e.g., Image > Stacks > Z Project), and image windows being actively used, rather than just running a headless script from the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually generated the required image types
integrity.visual_evidence: The intermediate Fiji windows should display visually distinct images corresponding to a Maximum projection, Average projection, XZ Reslice, and a grid Montage, rather than just duplicating the original file.

integrity.description: The agent actually measured the Z-profile
integrity.visual_evidence: A Fiji plot or results table containing the Z-axis profile data should be visible on screen before saving, proving the CSV was not just fabricated or copied from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Fly Brain' sample is a built-in Fiji/ImageJ image accessible via File > Open Samples. It is a real confocal microscopy image of a Drosophila brain showing nc82 antibody staining (green) and GAL4 driver line 78y expression (red). The original dimensions are approximately 256x256x57.
